TS-31.title: Fix status code mismatch + shell injection in tmux-claude-status
TS-31.acceptance_criteria: Line 298 case pattern includes `session_key_expired`; no bare `expired` as standalone alternative; lines 22 and 27 pass pidfile via sys.argv instead of string interpolation; no `open('$pidfile')` or `open("$pidfile")` remains; script executable; bash -n passes
TS-31.design_section:
### tmux-claude-status (modified)

- **Purpose:** Render tmux status line 0 (unchanged); fetch quota data from server (new)
- **Interfaces:** Called by tmux via `#(tmux-claude-status <pane_pid>)` every 5 seconds

### Error Signaling

When the server encounters an error condition, it returns the response with `"X"` string values for utilization instead of numbers. The renderer displays these as-is (e.g., `X%`).

| Server Condition | `status` field | `utilization` value |
|-----------------|---------------|-------------------|
| Session key expired | `"expired"` | `"X"` |

The `error` field contains a machine-readable error code (never raw exception text).

TS-33.title: Shell injection via filename in polyglot script (combined with TS-31)
TS-33.acceptance_criteria: Lines 22 and 27 pass pidfile via sys.argv instead of string interpolation; no `open('$pidfile')` or `open("$pidfile")` remains; script executable; bash -n passes
TS-33.design_section:
### tmux-claude-status (modified)

Same file as TS-31. Shell injection in pidfile interpolation at lines 22 and 27 — python3 -c invocations must use sys.argv[1] and shell must pass "$pidfile" as positional argument.

TS-32.title: Add non-root USER to Dockerfile
TS-32.acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile contains RUN useradd for non-root user; USER directive after pip install and before ENTRYPOINT; test_deploy.py verifies USER directive; pytest passes
TS-32.design_section:
### Security Considerations

The Dockerfile must not run as root. Add a non-root user (e.g., appuser) created via `useradd -r -s /usr/sbin/nologin appuser` and switch to it via `USER appuser` before the ENTRYPOINT.

TS-34.title: Make context hook use atomic writes
TS-34.acceptance_criteria: No bare writeFileSync(bridgePath, ...) to final path; write to tmp path + renameSync to final path; node -c passes; script remains executable

TS-35.title: Remove legacy quota scripts from repo and install.sh
TS-35.acceptance_criteria: scripts/tmux-status-quota-fetch deleted; scripts/tmux-status-quota-poll deleted; install.sh SCRIPTS array has neither; bash -n install.sh passes; SCRIPTS array retains 5 remaining scripts
TS-35.design_section:
### Server Replaces Standalone Poller

The scraping logic from `scripts/tmux-status-quota-fetch` is the canonical source, refactored into `server/tmux_status_server/scraper.py`. The old scripts are deprecated.

| Current script | Disposition |
|---------------|-------------|
| `tmux-status-quota-fetch` | Deprecated. Logic moves to `scraper.py` |
| `tmux-status-quota-poll` | Deprecated. Replaced by server's poll thread |

TS-37.title: Add interval lower bound validation in server config
TS-37.acceptance_criteria: parse_args() rejects --interval < 30 via parser.error(); --interval 29 raises SystemExit; --interval 30 succeeds; --interval 1 raises SystemExit; test_config.py has rejection and acceptance tests; pytest passes
TS-37.design_section:
### tmux-status-server (new package)

- **Purpose:** Scrape claude.ai for quota data and serve it via HTTP REST API
- **Dependencies:** `bottle>=0.12.25`, `curl_cffi>=0.5`, Python `>=3.10`

The server's --interval flag controls scrape frequency (default 300s). Must enforce minimum of 30 seconds to prevent accidental DoS against claude.ai, matching the old MIN_INTERVAL = 30 guard.
